metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_1924
metadata.comment:
Rationale: this paper shows that downregulation of Drebrin A using antisense oligonucleotides reduces clustering of F-actin in dendritic spines and reduces the density of spines in hippocampal neurons, which could be restored by re-expression of Drebrin A. This suggests that Drebrin controls the organization of the actin cytoskeleton and thereby regulates spine morphogenesis. Experimental description: rat hippocampal cultures were used: "[...] hippocampi were dissected from embryonic 18-d-old Wistar rats [...]".

23/11/2017 Pim
- Overexpressed drebrin A is of rat species: "The cDNA of rat drebrin A tagged with enhanced green fluorescent protein (GFP) "
